methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Redwood Materials is a Series D climate and energy company founded by J.B. Straubel, currently valued at $6.0B post-money and having raised $1.0B+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Linamar, ticker LNZA) in the climate and energy sector, including companies such as KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier to the last post-money valuation of $6.0B, implies a forward valuation of $9.0B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector peers at Series C/D stage—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—yields a median valuation of $1.0B with a range of $700M to $2.0B.

The triangulation produces a low estimate of $850M, a median of $5.0B, and a high of $10.3B. The wide range reflects material divergence between stage-progression methodology, which anchors to Redwood's last round, and peer-median comparison, which suggests earlier-stage peers command lower valuations. Key uncertainties include the extent to which Redwood's capital intensity and market position justify a premium to the peer set, and whether stage-progression assumptions reflect realistic exit-stage multiples in this sector.
